Description: Astragalus alpinus var. arcticus genome assembly, chromosome: 6
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Viridiplantae; Streptophyta; Embryophyta; Tracheophyta; Spermatophyta; Magnoliopsida; eudicotyledons; Gunneridae; Pentapetalae; rosids; fabids; Fabales; Fabaceae; Papilionoideae; 50 kb inversion clade; NPAAA clade; Hologalegina; IRL clade; Galegeae; Astragalus
Assembly: This project presents the alternate haplotype genome assembly of the arctic alpine milk-vetch, Astragalus alpinus arcticus, from a sample provided by Thomas Marcussen. The data generation and assembly were carried out by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). The drAstAlpi3 assembly is based on 34x PacBio HiFi and 61x Arima Hi-C data. The HiFi reads were filtered for adapters using HiFiAdapterFilt and assembled with hifiasm together with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to create two sets of reads for scaffolding the assemblies, ensuring that reads for hap1 scaffolding did not contain k-mers unique to hap2, and vice versa. YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters and putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. The chromosomes, as confirmed by Hi-C data, are named according to size.

The HiFi reads were filtered for adapters using HiFiAdapterFilt and assembled with hifiasm together with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Viridiplantae; Streptophyta; Embryophyta; Tracheophyta; Spermatophyta; Magnoliopsida; eudicotyledons; Gunneridae; Pentapetalae; rosids; fabids; Fabales; Fabaceae; Papilionoideae; 50 kb inversion clade; NPAAA clade; Hologalegina; IRL clade; Galegeae; Astragalus
Assembly: The assembly drAstAlpi1.hap1.1 is based on 34x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This assembly is comprised of. two equally curated haplotypes. Note that the order and orientation of the contigs on Chromosome 8 are of uncertain order and orientation. The region for hap1 is from 35.6-56.4 Mbp and for hap2 it is from 33.5-53.4 Mbp.

Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Chordata; Craniata; Vertebrata; Euteleostomi; Archelosauria; Archosauria; Dinosauria; Saurischia; Theropoda; Coelurosauria; Aves; Neognathae; Neoaves; Strisores; Caprimulgiformes; Caprimulgidae; Caprimulginae; Caprimulgus
Assembly: The assembly bCapEur3.1 is based on 87x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Falcon-unzip, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, 10X based scaffolding with scaff10x, Arrow polishing assisted by Merfin, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA2. Finally, the assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.
